subject: ELA
title: Reading: Ocean Plastic Crisis
description: Persuasive text analysis and argument evaluation.
passage:
Our Oceans Are Drowning in Plastic

Every year, more than 8 million metric tons of plastic end up in the world's oceans. This plastic does not disappear. Instead, it breaks into tiny pieces called microplastics that last hundreds of years. Marine animals — from sea turtles to blue whales — mistake plastic for food, which can be deadly.

Many people believe recycling is enough to solve the problem. But experts disagree. Less than 9% of all plastic ever produced has been recycled. The rest ends up in landfills, is burned, or floats in our oceans.

The most effective solution, scientists argue, is to produce less plastic in the first place. Countries like Canada and the European Union have already banned single-use plastics such as straws and plastic bags. Studies show that when plastic bags were banned in Ireland, their use fell by over 90%.

Individuals can also make a difference. Choosing reusable water bottles, shopping bags, and containers reduces personal plastic waste significantly. Schools and businesses that adopt plastic-free policies send a powerful message to manufacturers.

The ocean sustains all life on Earth. Protecting it from plastic is not just an environmental issue — it is a matter of survival for every species on the planet, including us.

questions.q: What is the AUTHOR'S MAIN CLAIM in this passage?
options: We must reduce plastic production and change our habits
Plastic is not really harmful to oceans
questions.answer: We must reduce plastic production and change our habits
questions.explanation: The author argues for reducing plastic production and personal behaviour change, beyond just recycling.

questions.q: How many metric tons of plastic enter oceans each year?
questions.explanation: 'More than 8 million metric tons' per the passage.

questions.q: What evidence does the author use about Ireland?
options: Plastic bag use fell by over 90% after a ban
questions.answer: Plastic bag use fell by over 90% after a ban
questions.explanation: Studies show plastic bag use in Ireland fell by over 90% after the ban — used as evidence that bans work.

questions.q: What does the word MICROPLASTICS mean based on the passage?
options: Tiny pieces that plastic breaks into
questions.answer: Tiny pieces that plastic breaks into
questions.explanation: The passage defines it: plastic 'breaks into tiny pieces called microplastics.'

questions.q: What percentage of all plastic ever produced has been recycled?
questions.explanation: 'Less than 9% of all plastic ever produced has been recycled.'

questions.q: Which of the following is an OPINION in this passage?
options: 8 million metric tons of plastic enter oceans each year
Less than 9% of plastic has been recycled
Protecting the ocean is a matter of survival for every species
Plastic bags were banned in the EU
questions.answer: Protecting the ocean is a matter of survival for every species
questions.explanation: This is a strong claim/opinion — it cannot be measured as a fact.

questions.q: What technique does the author use in the FINAL paragraph?
questions.explanation: The final paragraph creates urgency by connecting ocean health to the survival of all life.

questions.q: Name ONE individual action the author suggests to reduce plastic waste. (3-5 words)
questions.explanation: The passage suggests reusable water bottles, bags, and containers as individual actions.

questions.q: Why does the author say recycling ALONE is NOT enough?
options: Less than 9% of plastic has ever been recycled
People don't know how to recycle
questions.answer: Less than 9% of plastic has ever been recycled
questions.explanation: The author uses this statistic to show recycling has not solved the problem at scale.

questions.q: The word SUSTAINS in 'The ocean sustains all life on Earth' most likely means...
questions.explanation: To sustain means to support, nourish, or keep something going.
